stats_fallback.lengthening_details: 24 m midsection at Fincantieri Palermo, 31 August - 9 November 2015; +193 passenger cabins, +59 crew cabins; Doremi Spray Park added
stats_fallback.builder: Chantiers de l'Atlantique (Alstom Marine), Saint-Nazaire, France

related.class_notes: Lirica Class (first-in-class). Also historically called the Mistral Class because the design derives from Festival Cruises' Mistral. All four ships received 24m Renaissance Programme lengthening at Fincantieri Palermo between 2014-2015.

deployment.summer_2026: Eastern Mediterranean from Venice (Marghera) and Ancona — 7-night round-trips to Kotor, Mykonos, Syros
deployment.winter_2026_27: Eastern Mediterranean (CANCELLED 2026-27 Brazil/South America season in March 2026 for operational reasons; redeployed to Eastern Med)
deployment.not_south_africa: MSC Lirica is NOT sailing to South Africa. That deployment is MSC Armonia replacing MSC Opera from 1 November 2026.

history.christened: 2003 by Sophia Loren (her first MSC christening)
history.lengthening: 31 August - 9 November 2015 at Fincantieri Palermo (24m midsection, +193 passenger cabins, +59 crew cabins, Doremi Spray Park, Kaito Sushi Bar, expanded La Pergola cafeteria)
history.brazil_cancellation: March 2026 - MSC cancelled planned 2026-27 South America/Brazil winter season
